sourceMovie.title: Joker
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Joker (2019) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Joker clicked for you, American Psycho (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: I Care a Lot
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Joker clicked for you, I Care a Lot (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and thriller, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Joker?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Promising Young Woman, I Care a Lot—each shares DNA with Joker while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Joker?
faq.answer: Joker (2019) is best described as Crime, Thriller, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Joker part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Joker Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
